---
title: "Updated DC Crime Data"
author: "Ian Sargent"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Packages
```{r}
# Load packages with pacman library
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data Manipulation
  sf, # Working with Simple Feature Geometries 
  janitor, # Data Cleaning
  pscl, # Zero-Inflated NB Model
  MASS, # For Negative Binomial Regression Model
  pscl,
  grid, # Rootogram Customization
  GWmodel # For Geographically Weighted Regression 
)
```


### Load Datasets

#### Census
```{r}
# Raw Census Dataset
census <- st_read("Census_ACS_new.geojson") |> 
  # Put into Wide Format
  pivot_wider(
    # Tract x year combinations
    id_cols = c(tract, year, geometry),
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = estimate
  )
```


#### Crime Incidents
```{r}
# Load Crime Incident Datasets by Year
years <- 2017:2024
crime <- map_dfr(years, ~{
  url <- paste0("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/main/Data/Crime/Crime_Incidents_in_", .x, ".geojson")
  st_read(url) |> mutate(year = .x)
})
```


#### Metro Stations
```{r}
# Load Geospatial DC Metro Stations
metro <- st_read(dsn = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/refs/heads/main/Data/Metro/Metros_Ground_Transfer.geoJson") |> 
  dplyr::select(c(NAME, LINE, ADDRESS, GIS_ID, Above_Ground, Transfer_Station, geometry)) |> 
  janitor::clean_names()
```


### Ensure Coordinate Systems
```{r}
# Changing CRS for each dataset
metro <- st_transform(metro, crs=4326)
census <- st_transform(census, crs=4326)
crime <- st_transform(crime, crs=4326)

# CRS Validation
st_crs(metro) == st_crs(census)
st_crs(metro) == st_crs(crime)
st_crs(crime) == st_crs(census)
```


### Tract-Level Metro Proximity
```{r} 
# Define Tract Centroids (using the 2020 boundaries)
tract_centroids <- st_centroid(census |> filter(year == 2020)) 

# Standardize CRS for distance variables
tract_centroids <- st_transform(tract_centroids, crs=26918)
metro_projected <- st_transform(metro, crs=26918)

# Distance from each centroid to all metro stations
dist_matrix_tracts <- st_distance(tract_centroids, metro_projected)

# Assign nearest station info to tracts
nearest_idx_tracts <- apply(dist_matrix_tracts, 1, which.min)
tract_centroids$dist_to_nearest_metro <- apply(dist_matrix_tracts, 1, min)
tract_centroids$nearest_station_id <- metro_projected$gis_id[nearest_idx_tracts]

# Define Distance Buffer Variables (400m and 800m)
tract_centroids$station_within_400m <- as.integer(tract_centroids$dist_to_nearest_metro <= 400)
tract_centroids$station_within_800m <- as.integer(tract_centroids$dist_to_nearest_metro <= 800)


# Join station attributes with
tract_proximity_to_metro <- tract_centroids |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(metro_projected), by = c("nearest_station_id" = "gis_id")) |>
  dplyr::select(tract, dist_to_nearest_metro, nearest_station_id, station_within_400m, station_within_800m, line, above_ground, transfer_station) |> 
  arrange(tract)
```


### Joining Tables
```{r}
# Join Crime Incidents With Census (Incidents Within Each Tract)
tract_crime <- st_join(crime, census, join = st_within)

# Clean the Dataset
crimes_census <- tract_crime |> 
  # Remove rows where years don't match up
  filter(year.x == year.y) |> 
  # Rename the year column
  rename("year" = "year.y")|> 
  # Remove unnecessary variables
  dplyr::select(-c(BLOCK, BLOCK_GROUP, WARD, ANC, DISTRICT, PSA, VOTING_PRECINCT, BID, OCTO_RECORD_ID, year.x)) |> 
  janitor::clean_names()
```


### Distance to Metro Stations
```{r}
# Ensure CRS of Combined and Metro Datasets
crimes_census_proj <- st_transform(crimes_census, crs = 26918)
metro_proj <- st_transform(metro, crs = 26918)

# Generate the data matrix to find nearest station from each centroid
dist_matrix <- st_distance(crimes_census_proj, metro_proj)
nearest_idx <- apply(dist_matrix, 1, which.min)

crimes_census$dist_to_nearest <- apply(dist_matrix, 1, min)
crimes_census$nearest_station_id <- metro$gis_id[nearest_idx]
crimes_census$nearest_station_name <- metro$name[nearest_idx]
```


### Create Distance Buffer Variables
```{r}
crimes_census$station_within_400m <- as.integer(crimes_census$dist_to_nearest <= 400)
crimes_census$station_within_800m <- as.integer(crimes_census$dist_to_nearest <= 800)
```


### Join Metro to the Master Dataset
```{r}
# Combined Master Dataset
master <- crimes_census |>
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(metro_proj), by = c("nearest_station_id" = "gis_id")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(xblock, yblock, objectid, address, name, census_tract, neighborhood_cluster)) |> 
  mutate(
    month = month(report_dat),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
      month %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "Spring",
      month %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "Summer",
      month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"),
    red_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "red")),
    green_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "green")),
    yellow_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "yellow")),
    blue_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "blue")),
    orange_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "orange")),
    silver_line = as.integer(str_detect(line, "silver"))
  )
```


```{r}
# Define violent vs property offenses
violent <- c("HOMICIDE", "ASSAULT W/DANGEROUS WEAPON", "SEX ABUSE", "ROBBERY")
property <- c("BURGLARY", "THEFT/OTHER", "THEFT F/AUTO", "MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT", "ARSON")

# Aggregate to tract-month
nb_data_geo <- master |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  filter(!is.na(tract)) |>
  group_by(tract, year, month) |>
  summarise(
    # Outcome variables
    total_crimes   = n(),
    violent_crimes = sum(offense %in% violent),
    property_crimes = sum(offense %in% property),
    
    # Metro proximity (constant within tract but carry through)
    dist_to_nearest     = first(dist_to_nearest),
    within_400m         = first(station_within_400m),
    within_800m         = first(station_within_800m),
    nearest_station_name = first(nearest_station_name),
    above_ground        = first(above_ground),
    transfer_station    = first(transfer_station),
    red_line            = first(red_line),
    green_line          = first(green_line),
    yellow_line         = first(yellow_line),
    blue_line           = first(blue_line),
    orange_line         = first(orange_line),
    silver_line         = first(silver_line),
    
    # Census socioeconomic controls (constant within tract-year)
    total_population         = first(total_population),
    median_household_income  = first(median_household_income),
    population_below_poverty = first(population_below_poverty),
    unemployed_population    = first(unemployed_population),
    bachelors_degree         = first(bachelors_degree),
    masters_degree           = first(masters_degree),
    professional_degree      = first(professional_degree),
    total_housing_units      = first(total_housing_units),
    doctorate_degree         = first(doctorate_degree),
    vacant_units             = first(vacant_units),
    renter_occupied_units    = first(renter_occupied_units),
    pct_non_hispanic_black   = first(non_hispanic_black / total_population),
    pct_hispanic_latino      = first(hispanic_latino / total_population),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  # Log population offset for NB model
  mutate(
    # Population rates
    pct_black_nh       = pct_non_hispanic_black,
    pct_hispanic       = pct_hispanic_latino,
    pct_poverty        = population_below_poverty / total_population,
    pct_unemployed     = unemployed_population / total_population,
    pct_bachelors_only = bachelors_degree / total_population,
    pct_graduate      =(masters_degree + professional_degree + doctorate_degree) / total_population,
    
    # Household rates
    pct_renter        = renter_occupied_units / total_housing_units,
    pct_vacant        = vacant_units / total_housing_units,
    log_pop = log(total_population)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(pct_non_hispanic_black, pct_hispanic_latino,
                   population_below_poverty, unemployed_population,
                   vacant_units, total_housing_units, renter_occupied_units,
                   bachelors_degree, masters_degree, 
                   professional_degree, doctorate_degree))
```




```{r}
# 1. Distribution of outcome variables
nb_data |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(total_crimes, violent_crimes, property_crimes),
               names_to = "type", values_to = "count") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = count)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue") +
  facet_wrap(~type, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crime Counts (Tract-Month)", x = "Count", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# 2. Zero inflation check
nb_data |>
  summarise(
    pct_zero_total    = mean(total_crimes == 0),
    pct_zero_violent  = mean(violent_crimes == 0),
    pct_zero_property = mean(property_crimes == 0)
  )
```


```{r}
# 3. Overdispersion check (mean vs variance)
nb_data |>
  summarise(
    across(c(total_crimes, violent_crimes, property_crimes),
           list(mean = mean, var = var), .names = "{.col}_{.fn}")
  )
```


```{r}
# 4. Crime trends over time
nb_data |>
  group_by(year, month) |>
  summarise(total = sum(total_crimes), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(paste(year, month, "01", sep = "-"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = total)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Total Crime Over Time (Tract-Month Aggregated)", x = "", y = "Total Crimes") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# 5. Crime by metro proximity
nb_data |>
  filter(!is.na(within_800m)) |>
  mutate(proximity = factor(within_800m, labels = c("Outside 800m", "Within 800m"))) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(total_crimes, violent_crimes, property_crimes),
               names_to = "type", values_to = "count") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = proximity, y = count, fill = proximity)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~type, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Crime Counts by Metro Proximity", x = "", y = "Count", fill = "") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# 6. Distance to metro vs crime count
nb_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = dist_to_nearest, y = total_crimes)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Distance to Nearest Metro vs Total Crimes",
       x = "Distance (meters)", y = "Total Crimes (tract-month)") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# 7. Correlation matrix of predictors
nb_data |>
  dplyr::select(pct_poverty, pct_unemployed, pct_black_nh, pct_hispanic, pct_renter, pct_vacant,
                pct_bachelors_only, pct_graduate, median_household_income, dist_to_nearest) |>
  cor(use = "complete.obs") |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("var1") |>
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "corr") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = var1, y = var2, fill = corr)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(corr, 2)), size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Predictor Correlation Matrix", fill = "r")
```

```{r}
# 8. Key socioeconomic predictors vs crime
nb_data |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pct_poverty, pct_bachelors_only, pct_vacant, median_household_income),
               names_to = "predictor", values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = total_crimes)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~predictor, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Socioeconomic Predictors vs Total Crimes", x = "", y = "Total Crimes") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
# Negative Binomial Count Models

# Predicting Violent Crimes
nb_violent <- glm.nb(
  violent_crimes ~ within_400m + above_ground + transfer_station +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop),
  data = nb_data
) |> summary()

# Predicting Property Crimes
nb_property <- glm.nb(
  property_crimes ~ within_400m + within_800m + above_ground + transfer_station +
    median_household_income + pct_poverty + pct_unemployed +
    pct_black_nh + pct_hispanic + pct_renter + pct_vacant +
    pct_bachelors_only + offset(log_pop),
  data = nb_data
) |> summary()
```


```{r}
violent_or <- data.frame(Violent_ORs = round(exp(coef(nb_violent)), 4))
property_or <- data.frame(Property_ORs = round(exp(coef(nb_property)), 4))

violent_or$Variable <- rownames(violent_or)
violent_or$OR_violent <- violent_or$Violent_ORs
violent_or <- violent_or |> dplyr::select(-Violent_ORs)
rownames(violent_or) <- NULL

property_or$Variable <- rownames(property_or)
property_or$OR_property <- property_or$Property_ORs
property_or <- property_or |> dplyr::select(-Property_ORs)
rownames(property_or) <- NULL


odd_ratios <- left_join(violent_or, property_or, by="Variable")
odd_ratios
```

```{r}
# Observed frequency table
observed_v <- table(nb_data$violent_crimes)
observed_p <- table(nb_data$property_crimes)

# Distinct count values
counts_v <- as.numeric(names(observed_v))
counts_p <- as.numeric(names(observed_p))

# Predicted means
mu_hat_v <- predict(nb_violent, type = "response")
mu_hat_p <- predict(nb_property, type = "response")

# NB dispersion parameter
theta_v <- nb_violent$theta
theta_p <- nb_property$theta

# Expected frequencies for each count value
fitted_v <- sapply(counts_v, function(k) {
  sum(dnbinom(k, size = theta_v, mu = mu_hat_v))})

fitted_p <- sapply(counts_p, function(k) {
  sum(dnbinom(k, size = theta_p, mu = mu_hat_p))})
```


```{r}
# Rootogram showing fitted values of the model vs actual counts (in sqrt frequency)
# SOURCE: Kleiber, C., and Zeileis, A. (2016). Visualizing count data regressions using rootograms. Available at: http://arxiv.org/abs/1605.01311.

vcd::rootogram(
  observed_v, 
  fitted_v, 
  xlab = "Violent Crime Incidents", 
  type="hanging", 
  scale="sqrt", 
  points_gp = gpar(fontsize=8, col="red"),
  main="Violent Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts",
  main_gp = gpar(fontsize=14, fontface="bold"), 

)

vcd::rootogram(
  observed_p, 
  fitted_p, 
  xlab = "Property Crime Incidents", 
  type="hanging", 
  scale="sqrt", 
  points_gp = gpar(fontsize=0, col="red"),
  main="Property Crimes: Fitted vs Actual Counts",
  main_gp = gpar(fontsize=14, fontface="bold"), 
)
```


```{r}

glimpse(nb_data)


```
